headline: South Florida tech scene heating up—eMed's massive Series A & more major moves this week

stories.title: Tom Brady-backed eMed raises $200M Series A, hits $2B valuation
stories.summary: Miami telehealth startup eMed just closed a huge $200M Series A led by Aon Consulting with Brady, Linda Yaccarino & biotech investor Jeff Aronin backing it. They're using AI to manage GLP-1 meds coverage & chronic disease stuff for corporate health plans.
stories.takeaway: GLP-1 and chronic disease management are lowkey printing money—eMed's bet on agentic AI for employers and insurers is fr validating this entire market

stories.title: XFX closes $17M Series A for stablecoin forex trading infrastructure
stories.summary: Miami-based XFX landed $17M Series A from Castle Island Ventures to speed up real-time forex trades between fiat & stablecoins. They're basically fixing the problem of settlements taking days.
stories.takeaway: Crypto infrastructure is getting serious—XFX solved a real pain point in the stablecoin market when banks are slow AF

stories.title: IDB opens first US office outside DC in Miami, bet on Latin America deals
stories.summary: The Inter-American Development Bank just opened its Miami office focused on private investment & connecting Latin American opportunities with global investors. Major win fr the region's capital hub positioning.
stories.takeaway: Miami's officially becoming the HQ for Americas capital—IDB's move signals institutional money is realizing what startups already knew

stories.title: Boca Raton approves $500K incentive package for D-Wave quantum computing HQ
stories.summary: D-Wave, the publicly traded quantum computing company, is setting up HQ in Boca Raton & just got a $500K economic incentive from the city council. Moving quantum tech to SoFla tbh
stories.takeaway: South Florida's landing serious deep tech infrastructure—quantum computing in Boca is a vibe that changes the region's startup DNA

watchNext.title: Palantir HQ move to Miami signals mega-shift in AI company geography
watchNext.why: The $300B AI giant moved from Denver to Miami—literally the largest publicly traded company in South Florida now. Shows the capital gravity is shifting from Silicon Valley to Florida fr

watchNext.title: Big tech's $650B AI spending wave could ripple into South Florida jobs & infrastructure
watchNext.why: Amazon ($200B), Google ($185B), Meta ($135B) spending crazy on AI means demand for talent, engineering, logistics in emerging hubs like South Florida. Lowkey important for the whole ecosystem's next phase
